name: ClaimsLogik Property Claims Automation API
what_it_does: ClaimsLogik property insurance claims automation REST API for streamlined claim intake, repair estimate coordination, and claims lifecycle management. Enables AI agents to manage first notice of loss (FNOL) intake and claim creation for claims initiation automation, handle contractor estimate submission and desk review for claims cost management, access claim status tracking and communication for claimant experience automation, retrieve repair scope and line-item estimate review for claims audit automation, manage independent adjuster assignment and field inspection scheduling for claims operations, handle total loss determination and salvage management for auto and property claims, access subrogation identification and recovery workflow for claims recovery automation, retrieve claims analytics and cycle time reporting for claims performance monitoring, manage reinsurance reporting and catastrophe claims workflow for large loss operations, and integrate ClaimsLogik with core insurance systems, repair networks, and catastrophe management platforms.
use_cases: Processing FNOL intake from claims initiation agents
Reviewing repair estimates from claims audit agents
Tracking claim status from claimant communication agents
Identifying subrogation from claims recovery agents
not_for: Life and health insurance without property claims focus
Small claims without automated repair network
Self-insured retention without claims administration structure
best_when: A property and casualty insurer, third-party administrator (TPA), or managing general agent wanting AI agents to automate FNOL intake, repair estimate review, contractor network coordination, and claims cycle time management.
avoid_when: STATE INSURANCE CLAIMS REGULATION: Automated claim handling must comply with state unfair claims settlement practice acts (UCSPA); automated claim denial, delay, or underpayment triggers UCSPA investigation and bad faith exposure in every state of operation. Bad faith claims handling — automated claim decisions without adequate investigation create insurance bad faith liability; automated claim denial without reasonable investigation and documentation creates punitive damage exposure. Privacy of medical and loss records — claim files contain PII and PHI; automated claims processing must implement HIPAA minimum necessary and state privacy protections; automated exposure of claim information creates regulatory and civil liability. Total loss determination accuracy — automated total loss decisions based on incorrect ACV or repair cost calculation create claimant dispute and state department of insurance complaint risk. Catastrophe claims surge — automated claims systems must scale for catastrophe events; automated systems that fail during catastrophe surge create UCSPA timeline violation across mass claims volume.

auth.notes: ClaimsLogik uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Property claims automation for P&C insurers and TPAs. Founded 2016. Private. Claims intake, estimate review, and repair network coordination. Competes with Snapsheet, Hi Marley, and Guidewire ClaimCenter for claims automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-claim or enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-claim transaction pricing; enterprise annual subscription
pricing.notes: Private. Founded 2016. P&C insurance claims automation. Per-claim pricing model. TPA and insurer focus.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; catastrophe surge capacity documented in SLA

known_agent_gotchas: STATE UCSPA TIMELINE COMPLIANCE: Automated claims handling must comply with state unfair claims settlement practice act timelines (acknowledge within 10-15 days, accept/deny within 30-45 days depending on state); automated delays that miss state UCSPA deadlines create regulatory bad faith exposure

Bad faith investigation requirement — automated claim denial without documented adequate investigation creates insurance bad faith liability and punitive damage exposure; always implement human review gate before automated denial
Catastrophe surge capacity — automated claims systems must scale for CAT events when claim volume spikes 10-100x; validate SLA surge capacity before CAT season deployment

Total loss ACV accuracy — automated ACV calculation for total loss determination must use defensible valuation methodology (CCC, Mitchell, or market comparable); automated total loss with incorrect ACV creates claimant dispute and DOI complaint

Subrogation identification timing — automated subrogation identification must occur before claim payment release; automated payment without subrogation hold releases potential recovery; implement subrogation check in pre-payment workflow

Claim file documentation — automated claims handling must maintain complete claim file documentation for regulatory examination; automated actions without audit trail create state DOI examination failure
agent_readiness.security_notes: Insurance claims. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Claims and PII data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Insurance claims. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Claims and personal data.
